title: DNS Cache Poisoning
description: Injecting malicious DNS records into a resolver's cache to redirect users to attacker-controlled servers.
fullExplanation: DNS resolvers cache responses to reduce latency. If an attacker can inject a fake DNS response before the legitimate one arrives, the resolver caches the malicious record. All users of that resolver are then redirected to the attacker's IP — even though they typed the correct domain name. The Kaminsky attack (2008) made this practical by exploiting the predictable transaction IDs and source ports in DNS.

attackSteps.action: Attacker triggers resolver to query target domain
attackSteps.description: Send request for victim.com to target resolver

attackSteps.action: Race to respond before legitimate authoritative NS
attackSteps.description: Flood resolver with fake responses guessing 16-bit transaction ID

attackSteps.action: Fake response accepted if transaction ID matches
attackSteps.description: Probability: 1/65536 per attempt. Send thousands simultaneously.
victim.com → attacker's IP, cached for TTL duration

attackSteps.action: All users redirected to attacker's server
attackSteps.description: Attacker serves fake site, steals credentials, delivers malware

kaminskySolution: Randomize source port (adds 16 bits of entropy: 65536 × 65536 = 4 billion combinations). Patched in 2008.
keyFacts: Kaminsky attack (2008): exploited predictable DNS transaction IDs
Fix: randomize source port — adds 16 bits of entropy
DNSSEC: cryptographically signs DNS records — prevents poisoning
DNS over HTTPS (DoH): encrypts queries, prevents interception
Bailiwick checking: resolver only accepts records for queried domain
0x20 encoding: randomize case in query, verify same case in response

defenses.name: DNSSEC
defenses.description: Cryptographic signatures on DNS records. Resolver verifies signature chain from root.

defenses.name: DNS over HTTPS
defenses.description: Encrypts DNS queries in HTTPS — prevents interception and injection.

defenses.name: Source Port Randomization
defenses.description: Randomize UDP source port — adds 16 bits of entropy.

defenses.name: 0x20 Encoding
defenses.description: Randomize query case — response must match exact case.

defenses.name: Short TTL Monitoring
defenses.description: Alert on unusually short TTL values (attacker wants cache to expire quickly).
